with_skill: The eval only modified one dashboard (00-overview.json) but the test case design calls for two modified dashboards. The second dashboard (01-deployment-frequency) was not changed, so A4 expected_count of 2 doesn't match the actual 1 file produced. The assertion still passed because the skill correctly screenshotted only the affected dashboard — the test case input_context should be updated to match the actual eval setup.
The cosmetic annotation change does not produce a visually different dashboard. A better eval would modify a panel title or SQL query to create an observable before/after difference.
The skill's git stash flow could not be tested directly because the change was already committed. The file-swap approach is functionally equivalent but doesn't validate the exact stash/pop sequence.
This test was only validated indirectly. A proper eval requires a separate branch with no dashboard changes, which conflicts with the need for dashboard changes in tests 1 and 2.

overall_feedback: The with_skill evaluation produced real artifacts (actual PNG screenshots from a live Grafana instance) and honest grading. Key gaps: (1) without_skill comparison is completely missing — requires a second PR, (2) the before/after test used a change too cosmetic to produce a visual diff, (3) the no-dashboard test was indirect. These are documented honestly rather than papered over. A second iteration should address the visual-change gap; the without_skill comparison needs a separate agent session.
